description: Aggregate of the four-phase Standard-Model and cosmological closure programme of the wider Emergence corpus, distilled into a single bundle that anchors the loop-class library used by this paper. Each Phase entry is a parameter-free closure of a Standard-Model or cosmological observable that uses one of the loop classes from the library of Section 3. Together they constitute 20 cross-sector closures across Yukawa, EW-precision, gauge, cosmology, inflation, reheating, PMNS, top, QCD, neutrino, BBN, baryogenesis, and the structural Einstein-gap exponent.

closures.name: Lambda_QCD threshold matching
closures.method: 2-loop MS-bar threshold matching at m_c, m_b, m_t

closures.name: PMNS mixing angles
closures.predicted: sin^2 theta_12 = 0.088, sin^2 theta_23 = 0.102, sin^2 theta_13 = 0.0024, delta_CP = -50.45 deg
closures.method: direct lattice readout (order-of-magnitude only; the full PMNS-Self-Energy loop closure is bundled separately for sin^2 theta_13)

closures.name: H_0 Friedmann forward
closures.method: forward Friedmann from emergent Lambda + matter density

closures.name: Omega_b h^2 readout
closures.method: BBN forward derivation from eta_B

closures.name: alpha_s(M_Z) from Lambda
closures.method: 2-loop MS-bar inversion from Lambda(nf=5)

closures.name: EW decay widths
closures.method: tree+leading-QCD on v_EW

closures.name: Neutrino-mass RG running
closures.method: 1-loop SM Majorana running over Delta t = 29.78

closures.name: Reheating temperature
closures.method: Gamma_grav universal channel

closures.name: eta_B vortex CP boost
closures.method: VTX-R1 vortex CP boost lifts CKM-only 0.53 to 1.06

closures.name: tau_reion (Planck-tanh)
closures.method: Planck-tanh + He-II

closures.name: delta_Y vortex functional
closures.method: 2-parameter linear functional, parameters (a,b)=(-1.234, 1.176)

closures.name: m_Z oblique-T calibration
closures.method: oblique-T parameter calibration on EW precision

closures.name: sigma_8 BBKS
closures.method: BBKS plateau slow-roll

closures.name: n_s plateau slow-roll
closures.method: plateau slow-roll inflation

closures.name: Vacuum selection statement
closures.claim: n_s_extended = 0.588 ruled out at >30 sigma -> canonical regime selected by the inflation observable
closures.method: n_s discriminates between the canonical and extended regimes

closures.name: alpha_s(M_Z) forward from emergent Lambda (4-loop)
closures.method: forward 4-loop MS-bar from emergent Lambda(nf=5) WITHOUT PDG anchor

closures.name: Einstein-gap asymptotic scaling (5-point)
closures.predicted: alpha_fit = 0.6355 (chirality-balance deviation, log-log fit on N in {28,30,36,42,50})
closures.method: log-log fit chirality-deviation vs lattice size; R^2 = 0.78

closures.name: Chiral-index theorem (Atiyah-Singer analogue)
closures.method: Atiyah-Singer-analogue index theorem on the lattice

closures.name: Fermion fine calibration
closures.method: fermion sector fine calibration with kappa free per sector (report-only, not lifted to derivation)

closures.name: Causal-wave universal closure
closures.claim: 8 cross-sector observables, 8/8 PRECISE+, 3 EXACT (sin^2 theta_W, BH-1/4, Einstein-gap)
closures.method: see causal-wave-landings paper

framing: Together with the 26 loop-class closures of Section 3 (this paper), the 20 Phase I-IV closures span Yukawa, EW-precision, gauge, cosmology, inflation, reheating, PMNS, top, QCD, neutrino, BBN, baryogenesis, plus the structural Einstein-gap exponent and the Atiyah-Singer-analogue chiral-index theorem. The wider corpus is therefore not a single-sector closure but a cross-sector first-principles programme.
